Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

- Rows 21-23: Left panel top area (white), with gray area between panels (cols 24-32), then purple bar (cols 33-38)
- Rows 24-25: Same pattern
- Rows 26-29: Right panel has charcoal frame (ccccc at cols 44-48)
- Rows 30-32: Right panel has green frame with target (NNN at cols 42-44), and left panel has charcoal frame at bottom
- Rows 33-36: Left panel has charcoal frame (ccccc at cols 11-15)
- Rows 37-41: Bottom area
I see there's a gray (g) corridor between the left and right panels! Looking at rows 21-25, cols 24-32 are gray (g), and rows 33-36, cols 24-32 are also gray. But rows 26-32, cols 24-32 are white (w).
Wait, let me look more carefully:

- Left panel: cols 9-23 (wwwwwwwNNNwwwwww)

- Right panel: cols 39-53 (wwwwwwwwwwwwwww)

- Left panel: cols 9-32 (wwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwww) - all white, no gray gap!
- Purple bar: cols 33-38 (pppppp)
- Right panel: cols 39-53 (wwwNNNccccwwwww)
So the gray gap exists at rows 21-25 and 33-36, but at rows 26-32, the left panel extends all the way to col 32 (touching the purple bar).
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine. Looking at row 30:
`gwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwppppppwwwNNNccccwwwwwgg`
Counting from col 8:
- g at col 8
- w at cols 9-32 (24 white cells)
- p at cols 33-38 (6 purple cells)
- w at cols 39-41 (3 white cells)
- N at cols 42-44 (3 green cells)
- c at cols 45-48 (4 charcoal cells)
- w at cols 49-53 (5 white cells)
- g at cols 54-55
So at row 30, the left panel extends from col 9 to col 32, touching the purple bar. But at row 21:
`gwwwwwwNNNwwwwwwgggggggggppppppwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgg`
- g at col 8
- w at cols 9-14 (6 white cells)
- N at cols 15-17 (3 green cells)
- w at cols 18-23 (6 white cells)
- g at cols 24-32 (9 gray cells)
- p at cols 33-38 (6 purple cells)
- w at cols 39-53 (15 white cells)
- g at cols 54-55
So the left panel at row 21 only extends to col 23, and there's a gray gap from cols 24-32. This means the left panel has a narrower top section and a wider bottom section.
Looking at the layout more carefully:
- Left panel: cols 9-23 at rows 21-25 (narrow), cols 9-32 at rows 26-32 (wide), cols 9-23 at rows 33-41 (narrow)
- Wait, that's not right either. Let me look at row 33:
`gwwcwwwcwwwwwwgggggggggppppppwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgg`
- g at col 8
- w at cols 9-10 (2 white cells)
- c at col 11 (charcoal)
- w at cols 12-14 (3 white cells)
- c at col 15 (charcoal)
- w at cols 16-23 (8 white cells)
- g at cols 24-32 (9 gray cells)
- p at cols 33-38 (6 purple cells)
- w at cols 39-53 (15 white cells)
- g at cols 54-55
So the left panel at row 33 extends from col 9 to col 23, with a charcoal frame at cols 11-15. The gray gap is at cols 24-32.
Now I understand the layout better:
- Left panel: cols 9-23 at rows 21-25 and 33-41 (narrow sections), cols 9-32 at rows 26-32 (wide section with a bridge to the purple bar)
- The bridge at rows 26-32 connects the left panel to the purple bar
- Similarly, the right panel has a bridge at rows 26-32 connecting to the purple bar
- The gray gap at rows 21-25 and 33-36 separates the left and right panels
So the player can't pass through the purple bar directly. But maybe the player can go through the gray gap? Let me check if the player can move into the gray area.
Actually, wait. Looking at the initial state, the player started at (31, 19) in the left panel. The charcoal frame in the left panel is at [32,11]-[36,15]. The player moved right and got stuck at col 31, which is at the edge of the left panel's wide section.
Let me reconsider the game. Maybe the goal isn't to move the player to the target. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Move the player to specific locations to "paint" or "collect" something
2. Use MOUSE to swap colors between the two green frames
3. Complete some pattern
Or maybe the goal is to get the player's color to match the target's color, or vice versa.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me move the player to the right panel's charcoal frame location and see what happens. The right charcoal frame is at [26,44]-[30,48].
Actually, wait. I just realized something. The player is currently at (22, 16), which is in the left panel's narrow top section. The gray gap is at cols 24-32. Maybe I can move RIGHT from here to enter the gray gap, then continue right to the right panel!
Let me try moving RIGHT from the current position.

# Let me try moving RIGHT to see if the player can enter the gray gap
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
